description: DraftProvenance.user_utterance is hardcoded to empty string. R-08.2 requires the field populated; R-08.24 requires sanitization via project_to_schema when approver ≠ initiator. Neither population nor sanitization occurs.
evidence: draft-proposer.ts:59 → user_utterance: ''
suggested_action: Pass the user utterance into DraftProposer.propose(), apply project_to_schema when approver ≠ initiator, and add the taintSources population from TurnState.

description: DraftProvenance.derived_from_tainted_sources is always an empty array; TurnState.taintSources are never iterated to populate the field. R-08.2 requires all provenance fields populated.
evidence: draft-proposer.ts:57-63 — provenance built with derived_from_tainted_sources: [] unconditionally. No reference to turnState.taintSources.
suggested_action: Iterate turnState.taintSources (populated by plan 01 gateway taint-wrap) and map each entry into the derived_from_tainted_sources array.

description: Kernel audit events agent.draft_approved, agent.draft_rejected, and agent.draft_execution_failed are entirely absent from the codebase. R-08.28 requires all seven audit events. Only agent.draft_proposed (draft-sink), agent.draft_executed (execute-approved-draft), and agent.draft_expired (sweep-expired-drafts) are emitted.
evidence: grep across agents/ for 'agent.draft_approved', 'agent.draft_rejected', 'agent.draft_execution_failed' returns zero results.
suggested_action: Emit agent.draft_approved in the approval tRPC handler, agent.draft_rejected in the reject handler, and agent.draft_execution_failed in the execute-approved-draft error paths.

description: R-08.16 requires domain-revalidation of preconditions when approvalFreshness === 'revalidate'. The job payload carries approval_freshness but the worker never reads or acts on it — no revalidation step is performed regardless of the flag value.
evidence: execute-approved-draft.ts: approval_freshness field declared in job type at line 14; grep for 'revalidate' in the file returns only line 15 (type declaration) — no conditional logic exists.
suggested_action: Add a revalidation step in ExecuteApprovedDraftWorker.handle() that calls the domain command's revalidate() method when approval_freshness === 'revalidate'.

description: R-08.25 requires <AgentDraftCard> presenter component in @future/ui. The packages/ui/src/components directory has no draft-related or agent-related component. The §12 acceptance criterion 'AgentDraftCard presenter is the only rendering path' cannot be met.
evidence: ls packages/ui/src/components shows no draft or agent files. Search for 'AgentDraftCard' and 'agent-draft-card' returns no results under packages/ui/.
suggested_action: Implement AgentDraftCard in packages/ui/src/components/agent-draft-card.tsx per the §4 presenter contract.

description: R-08.36 requires MVP write scope enforced via feature flag: planner.* and projects.* enabled day 1; people.* gated behind feature.agent.people_writes (default off). No feature flag check or domain allowlist enforcement was found in the gateway or draft-proposer paths.
evidence: No reference to 'feature.agent.people_writes' or 'people_writes' exists in the agents module implementation files.
suggested_action: Implement domain allowlist check in the gateway pipeline (before tier classification) that refuses drafts for non-whitelisted domains when the flag is off.

description: agent_draft table has no FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY or CREATE POLICY in the migration. Plan §3 specifies RLS + tenant_id; plan §12 acceptance criterion requires cross-tenant isolation. Only agent_tool_result_cache has RLS enabled in the migration file.
evidence: 0000_initial.sql line 1832-1834: only agent_tool_result_cache gets FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY. agent_draft, agent_schedule, agent_schedule_run, agent_delegation tables lack equivalent statements.
suggested_action: Add FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY + tenant_id-based CREATE POLICY for agent_draft, agent_schedule, agent_schedule_run, and core.agent_delegation.

description: Plan 08 §8 requires metrics: agent_draft_proposed_total, agent_draft_approved_total, agent_draft_rejected_total, agent_draft_expired_total, agent_draft_executed_total, agent_permission_widened_between_draft_and_execute_total, agent_approval_inbox_depth, agent_draft_ttl_utilized_ratio. None of these are defined in gateway-metrics.ts or any other metrics file.
evidence: Grepping for 'agent_draft_proposed_total', 'agent_draft_approved', 'agent_approval_inbox' in agents/ returns zero hits.
suggested_action: Add plan 08 draft metrics to a new draft-metrics.ts file (or extend gateway-metrics.ts).

description: Plan 08 §8 span set (DRAFT:propose, DRAFT:tier-classify, DRAFT:delegation-mint, DRAFT:submit, EXECUTE_APPROVED_DRAFT:*) is absent from the observability surface.
evidence: No OTel span creation found in draft-proposer.ts, draft-tier-classifier.ts, approval-executor-delegation-minter.ts, draft-sink.ts, or execute-approved-draft.ts.
suggested_action: Instrument DraftProposer and ExecuteApprovedDraftWorker with the plan 08 span tree.

description: Integration test layers required by §11 are absent: taint bump end-to-end (tool returns tainted → tier bumped), utterance sanitization (approver ≠ initiator), permission widened/narrowed between draft and execute, TTL expiry with sweeper, compliance query facade cross-tenant isolation, and full E2E Playwright flow are not present.
evidence: Only unit-level mock tests found for draft-proposer, draft-tier-classifier, execute-approved-draft, sweep-expired-drafts. No integration specs found with real DB for plan 08.
suggested_action: Add plan 08 integration test suite per §11 scenarios.

description: Scheduled summary-lint job over recent drafts (R-08.25a) to flag drafts with raw-args summaries as P2 incidents is not implemented. §12 acceptance criterion: 'Summary-lint job verified on seeded corpus' is unmet.
evidence: No file matching 'draft-summary-lint', 'summary.*lint', or similar found in agents module.
suggested_action: Implement a scheduled lint job that scans recent agent_draft rows for summary field violations.

description: R-09.28 requires the event router to filter schedule matches by event.tenant_id === schedule.tenant_id BEFORE calling ScheduledTurnSpawner.spawn(). No such filter exists in the spawner or any observable event-router integration. No cross-tenant event filter or metric agent_event_router_cross_tenant_rejected_total is emitted.
evidence: scheduled-turn-spawner.ts receives schedule but never validates event tenant vs. schedule tenant. Grep for 'R-09.28', 'cross_tenant_rejected', 'agent_event_router' returns no hits in agents/.
suggested_action: Add event.tenant_id === schedule.tenant_id hard-match check in the event router before calling spawn(), plus emit agent_event_router_cross_tenant_rejected_total metric on mismatch.

description: R-09.6a requires the worker/gateway boundary to refuse any mutation intent for direct dispatch. The ScheduledTurnWorker emits only a dry-run audit and marks the run 'completed' without invoking the turn pipeline at all (MVP stub at line 100). The read-only policy envelope (refusing mutation tools) is not enforced as a runtime gate; it is simply absent.
evidence: scheduled-turn-worker.ts:100-126 shows the turn pipeline is NOT invoked; only a dry-run audit fires. The worker completes without actually executing the scheduled turn.
suggested_action: Wire the full turn pipeline into ScheduledTurnWorker with the read-only policy envelope. The current dry-run stub should become a conditional code path controlled by the feature flag, not the sole code path.

description: agent_schedule and agent_schedule_run tables have no FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY or CREATE POLICY in the migration. Plan §3 specifies tenant_id (RLS) on both tables.
evidence: 0000_initial.sql: agent_schedule at line 214, agent_schedule_run at line 197 — no ENABLE/FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY statements follow their CREATE TABLE blocks.
suggested_action: Add FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY + CREATE POLICY for both tables in the migration.

description: R-09.23 requires Admin UI shows all active grants per user. R-09 §4 ScheduleUiFacade requires canDo('admin.schedule.create') for tenant-wide creation and canDo('agent.schedule.create') for personal. Both permission gates are documented as 'deferred to future task' with no canDo check implemented.
evidence: schedule-ui-facade.ts:88: 'Permission gates (canDo checks) are deferred to a future task.'
suggested_action: Implement canDo permission checks in scheduleUiRouter create procedures before the plan ships to production.

description: Plan 09 §8 requires metrics: agent_schedule_fire_total, agent_schedule_active_count, agent_schedule_run_duration_ms, agent_delegation_active_count, agent_delegation_expired_total, agent_delegation_creations_total, agent_async_taint_seeded_total, agent_schedule_ceiling_exhausted_total, agent_schedule_consecutive_failure_pause_total, agent_schedule_tenant_spend_pause_total, agent_schedules_revoked_on_offboarding_total, agent_delegation_scope_drift_total, agent_event_router_cross_tenant_rejected_total. None are defined.
evidence: Grepping for any of these metric names in agents/ returns zero results.
suggested_action: Implement plan 09 schedule/delegation metrics in a new schedule-metrics.ts file.

description: Integration test scenarios from §11 are unimplemented: cross-tenant event-router tenant filter test (R-09.28); user offboarding flow (delegations revoked, schedules paused, audit inventory); delegation scope drift (warning-not-failure, narrowed scope); consecutive-failure escalation (3 runs → auto-pause); tenant active-schedule cap and tenant spend cap. These are all §12 acceptance criteria.
evidence: Scheduled-turn-spawner.spec.ts and scheduled-turn-worker.spec.ts contain only unit-level mocked tests. No cross-tenant, offboarding, or consecutive-failure integration specs found.
suggested_action: Add plan 09 integration test suite per §11 scenarios using real DB.

description: R-09.31 requires admin_tenant_config.max_active_schedules INT (default 100) with 80%/100% gate and R-09.32 requires admin_tenant_config.scheduled_spend_daily_limit_usd. Neither column is present in admin_tenant_config and no enforcement logic was found.
evidence: No reference to 'max_active_schedules' or 'scheduled_spend_daily_limit_usd' in agents/ application services.
suggested_action: Add columns to admin_tenant_config and implement enforcement in ScheduleRepository.create() and a daily aggregation job.

description: R-15.8 requires the PR review checklist bot to fire on any PR touching modules/*/agent/**. The GitHub Action uses the regex '^apps/api/src/modules/[^/]+/agent/' which is correct for the actual module path. However the plan specifies the EI-10 glob as modules/*/agent/** (module path, not full path). The regex correctly matches the actual path pattern — this is functionally correct but the regex does not also cover tools/lint/agent-authoring/ touches for the lint tooling itself.
evidence: pr-agent-review-bot.yml:24: AGENT_FILES=$(echo "$CHANGED_FILES" | grep -E '^apps/api/src/modules/[^/]+/agent/')
suggested_action: Minor: ensure lint tooling changes under tools/lint/agent-authoring/ also trigger the bot, or document the intentional exclusion.

description: R-15.7 severity is set to 'error' in config (EI-10 glob coverage) but there is no dedicated rule implementation for R-15.7 in the rules index. The runner's EI-10 glob test (synthetic module fixture) verifies the glob behavior through the integration test, but there is no explicit LintRule with id='R-15.7' that could emit a finding if the glob breaks.
evidence: rules/index.ts exports 9 rules; no R-15.7 rule exists. config.ts line 52 assigns severity 'error' to 'R-15.7' but no rule references this ID.
suggested_action: Either create a formal R-15.7 rule that validates the glob configuration, or remove the orphaned severity entry and rely solely on the synthetic-module integration test.

description: R-15.12 requires quarterly override audit; §12 requires override-audit tooling produces a per-rule count report against a fixture with seeded overrides. The audit-overrides.ts file exists but no quarterly cron CI setup is wired in .github/workflows/. The ci.yml only runs the per-PR lint.
evidence: No workflow file found that runs audit-overrides.ts on a schedule. ci.yml only runs 'bun run lint:agent-authoring'.
suggested_action: Add a scheduled GitHub Actions workflow (quarterly or monthly) that runs audit-overrides.ts and posts results to docs/agents/repeat-issues.md.

description: All rules currently set to 'warning' severity (R-15.1 through R-15.4, R-15.9) as intended by the two-week warning grace period (§13). Escalation date set to 2026-05-10. This is per-plan design.
evidence: config.ts lines 46-55: all rules except R-15.5, R-15.6, R-15.10, R-15.11 are 'warning'. escalationDate: '2026-05-10'.
suggested_action: No action required. Track escalation date (2026-05-10) to flip rules to 'error'.

description: ASYNC_AUTONOMOUS_WRITES_ENABLED = false is a correct MVP constant. Beta-gate (4 weeks incident-free + ≥95% approval-rate) not yet reached. Dry-run audit path correctly emits agent.async_dry_run_would_have_written for soak period data.
evidence: scheduled-turn-worker.ts:21: const ASYNC_AUTONOMOUS_WRITES_ENABLED = false
suggested_action: No action required at MVP. The flag flip to true requires joint Product + Security sign-off per §16.

description: handleUserOffboarding returns reassignedScheduleCount: 0 — schedule reassignment is explicitly Beta-gated per §4 and §17.
evidence: delegation-lifecycle.ts:237: reassignedScheduleCount: 0 with comment '0 at MVP — reassignment is Beta'
suggested_action: No action required at MVP.

description: Agent-proposed L3 writes (GA gate) and delegation-signed autonomous writes (plan 09 Beta gate) are correctly absent from plan 08 implementation. Per §17 these are explicit out-of-scope items.
evidence: Plan 08 §17 explicitly lists these as out of scope.
suggested_action: No action required.
